title: 10 best herbal decoctions for dry cough
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for dry cough are a natural remedy made by steeping herbs in hot water to create a soothing drink. These decoctions help alleviate dry, irritating coughs by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation in the throat. For example, thyme, honey, and ginger are popular herbal decoctions that have been used to calm coughs and ease congestion. By drinking these decoctions, people with dry cough can experience relief from persistent hacking and coughing, allowing them to sleep better, breathe easier, and enjoy daily activities without discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with dry cough because it provides a soothing and protective coating to the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the mucous membranes, allowing for easier removal of phlegm and mucus. This helps to relieve congestion and dryness, providing quick relief from uncomfortable coughing fits. Additionally, licorice decoctions can also help to reduce cough frequency by coating the throat and reducing the urge to cough.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry cough.

Expectorant and demulcent properties: The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, while its demulcent properties create a protective barrier that soothes and shields the mucous membranes, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve coughing.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Licorice root's antioxidant compounds help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by viral or bacterial infections, while its antimicrobial properties can help combat secondary infections that may contribute to dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: A triterpenoid saponin, licoricidin has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation associated with dry cough.

Licorice glycosides (mainly glycyrrhizin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the airways, thereby alleviating dry cough.

Flavonoids (mainly isoflavones and flavanones): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, making them beneficial for dry cough relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are widely used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help relieve dry cough.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are used to make decoctions for dry cough because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antitussive (cough-suppressing) effects.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make decoctions for dry cough due to their content of saponins, which are known for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in autumn after the first frost when the plant is fully mature.
Clean and dry 10g of glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 1l of water in a pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids then store the liquid in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The menthol content in peppermint soothes and relaxes the throat muscles, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by a dry cough. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and alleviating congestion often associated with dry coughs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can ease the discomfort and pain associated with dry cough.

Expectorant properties: The menthol content in peppermint decoctions helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion that can exacerbate dry cough.

Antitussive properties: Peppermint's calming effects on the mucous membranes and its ability to reduce inflammation can also help to soothe and calm the cough reflex, reducing the frequency and severity of dry cough episodes.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with dry cough by creating a cooling sensation in the throat, temporarily numbing the pain and reducing inflammation.
Limonene: Limonene has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve dry cough symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, providing relief from dry cough and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Most commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which help to relieve cough and congestion.
Stems: Used for their strong, pungent flavor and aroma, which can help to break down and clear mucus from the airways.
Rhyzomes: Utilized for their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve congestion, thanks to their rich content of menthol and other essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers from a healthy plant in the morning.
Weigh 2-3 grams of the harvested material and chop it into small pieces to release oils.
Combine the chopped plant material with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has cooled down to room temperature.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with dry cough because it contains compounds such as thymol, which have natural expectorant properties. When consumed as a warm drink or added to food, thyme decoctions help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, providing relief from stubborn dry coughs. Additionally, thyme's anti-inflammatory effects may also help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the throat and chest, further soothing the discomfort associated with dry coughing.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Thyme's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe.

Antimicrobial property: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause or exacerbate dry coughs, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the respiratory tract.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and airways, providing relief from scratchiness and discomfort associated with dry coughing.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as a bronchodilator and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to relax the airway muscles and reduce inflammation, thereby easing dry cough symptoms.
Carvacrol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the dry cough, while also providing decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects.

Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antitussive (cough-suppressing) and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce cough reflexes and alleviate inflammation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly thymol, which has antimicrobial properties that help soothe and relieve dry cough.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are often used for their fragrance and flavor, and they contain thymol, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and cough symptoms.

Roots: Thymus vulgaris roots are sometimes used due to their ability to retain thymol, which is then released as the plant ages, providing a prolonged effect in treating dry cough and other respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 tablespoons of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves for decoction.
Combine the fresh or dried thymus vulgaris with 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Reduce heat to a simmer and steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Store the herbal thymus vulgaris decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The herb contains compounds that help to break down phlegm and reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs. This can provide relief from a dry, hacking cough by thinning out mucus and making it easier to expel. Additionally, purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe an irritated throat and reduce coughing spasms.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Purple coneflower contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect irritated mucous membranes in the throat, providing relief from dry cough symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower decoctions help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to dry coughs and other respiratory issues.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, may help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat and airways, providing relief from a persistent dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Ishagarin: A glycoside found in Echinacea purpurea, isharagrin helps to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of dry cough.
Cichoric acid: A phenolic compound in Echinacea purpurea, cichoric acid exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve dry cough symptoms.

Alkylamides: A group of compounds in Echinacea purpurea, alkylamides have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune system's response to respiratory infections and alleviate symptoms of dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in echinacoside and alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe dry coughs.
Leaves: High in phenolic acids, flavonoids, and rosmarinic acid, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in cough relief.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids, alkylamides, and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help alleviate dry coughs.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 to 60 grams of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the dried echinacea roots and flowers in a large pot with 1 liter of water.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 5 to 10 minutes or until the liquid is boiling.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes or until it has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract. The plant's diuretic properties also help to thin and reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, providing rapid relief from dry, hacking coughs. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with persistent coughing.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion decoction's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, soothing a dry cough caused by irritation.

Mucilages and saponins: The mucilage content in dandelion helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes, while its saponins have expectorant properties that loosen and clear out mucus, relieving congestion and coughing.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and taraxerin, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms of dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene helps with dry cough by reducing inflammation in the airways and exhibiting expectorant properties, making it easier to cough up mucus.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): These phenolic compounds help with dry cough by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune response, and exhibiting antitussive properties, which can help alleviate coughing.

Inulin: This polysaccharide helps with dry cough by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can improve the body's immune response and reduce inflammation, ultimately helping to alleviate coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain inulin, a natural expectorant that helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving dry cough.
Leaves: The leaves contain taraxasterol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an irritated respiratory tract and reduce coughing.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have a soothing effect on the respiratory system, helping to calm a dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 to 60 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces to release their active compounds.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 liters of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer for 10 to 15 minutes to allow the decoction to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest. The decoction's warming nature also helps to loosen and expel mucus, providing relief from congestion and discomfort. Additionally, ginger has natural expectorant properties that stimulate the body's natural clearance mechanisms, helping to bring up and clear out excess mucus and phlegm. This combination of anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties makes ginger decoctions a popular remedy for soothing and alleviating dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Ginger decoctions help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from dry, hacking coughs by facilitating the expulsion of phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to a dry, irritating cough by soothing irritated mucous membranes.
Antitussive properties: The phenolic compounds in ginger decoctions have been shown to suppress the cough reflex, providing relief from dry, persistent coughs by reducing the urge to cough.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These gingerols, particularly [6]-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, making it easier to relieve dry cough symptoms.
Shogaols: Shogaols, also found in ginger, have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections responsible for dry coughs, such as the common cold or flu.

Zingibain: Zingibain, a proteolytic enzyme found in ginger, has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help break down and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, thereby relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for decoctions, due to their high concentration of active compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used in decoctions to treat dry cough, as they contain similar active compounds to the rhyzomes.
Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale can be used in decoctions to treat dry cough, although they are less commonly used than the rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 inches of fresh zingiber officinale root for maximum flavor and potency.
Peel the root using a vegetable peeler to remove the outer layer.
Chop the peeled root into small pieces using a sharp knife for easier decoction.
Combine 1 teaspoon of chopped root with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with dry cough because they provide a soothing and moisturizing effect on the throat and lungs. The natural compounds present in elder flowers, such as flavonoids and triterpenes, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce congestion and ease irritation. Additionally, elder decoctions are rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the mucous membranes from damage caused by dry air or irritants, leading to a reduction in coughing episodes. This natural remedy can provide relief from dry cough symptoms without causing harm.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Elder decoction's antitussive properties help to soothe and calm a dry, irritated cough by reducing the urge to cough, allowing the body to rest and recover.
Mucolytic properties: Elder's mucolytic properties help to break down thick mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and discomfort associated with dry coughs.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and airways, providing relief from scratchiness, soreness, and discomfort caused by dry coughs.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help to reduce inflammation and relax the airways, making it easier to cough up mucus and alleviate dry cough symptoms.

Phenylethylamine alkaloid (sambunigrin and others): These alkaloids have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the lungs, relieving dry cough.

Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated mucous membranes, making it easier to breathe and alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve dry cough.
Leaves: Containing compounds like saponins and terpenoids, which have been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues and ease coughs.
Fruits: The berries of Sambucus nigra are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which contribute to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25g of dried sambucus nigra berries in a clean and dry container for later use.
Crush the berries using a mortar and pestle to release their medicinal properties within 5 minutes.
Combine the crushed berries with 250ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes in a warm and draft-free environment to allow infusion.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop decoctions helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties. The soothing extracts from the herb help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm that can irritate the throat and airways, providing fast relief from stubborn dry coughs. Additionally, hyssop's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, allowing for easier breathing and a more comfortable coughing experience. This natural remedy is a safe and effective way to alleviate dry cough symptoms without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop's saponins and flavonoids help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, while also reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve a dry, hacking cough.

Mucolytic activity: Hyssop's mucolytic compounds break down thickened mucus into smaller particles, making it easier to expectorate and relieving congestion and discomfort associated with a dry cough.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Hyssop's terpenes and flavonoids exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activity, helping to combat the underlying infection causing the dry cough and prevent further complications.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve cough symptoms by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Hyssopin: A terpenoid compound that helps relax the respiratory tract muscles, reduce spasms, and ease coughing, making it easier to breathe.
Urospermaldehyde: A sesquiterpene compound that has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thus relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of volatile oils, which have expectorant properties that help relieve dry cough.
Stems: The stems are utilized for their antitussive and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing a dry cough.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help to calm and relieve a dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested hyssopus officinalis in a single layer at 40°c for 2 to 3 hours to preserve the plant's properties.
Combine one teaspoon of dried hyssopus officinalis with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow decoctions helps with dry cough because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the mucous membranes in the throat. The anti-inflammatory properties of mallow help to reduce swelling and irritation, allowing for easier expectoration of phlegm and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, the demulcent nature of mallow creates a protective barrier that coats the throat, shielding it from further irritation and promoting healing. This natural remedy is a gentle and effective way to provide relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Mallow decoctions contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort associated with a dry cough.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The decoction contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to chronic dry coughs by reducing swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs.

Demulcent properties: Mallow's demulcent properties help form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, providing a soothing and calming effect that can help quiet a dry cough and promote easier breathing.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation that contribute to dry cough.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Malva sylvestris, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and alleviate coughing.
Saponins: Saponins in Malva sylvestris have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and relieving cough.
Flowers: The flowers are a good source of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, aiding in the relief of dry cough.
Roots: The roots of Malva sylvestris contain mucilage and glycosides, which help to reduce inflammation and provide relief from dry cough by thinning mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried flowers of malva sylvestris and store them in an airtight container.
Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain it using a fine mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and store the resulting decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
Consume 50-100ml of the cooled decoction as needed to promote relaxation and soothe inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, also have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce irritation and inflammation in the throat and respiratory tract, providing relief from a dry, hacking cough. Additionally, fennel's soothing properties can help calm irritated mucous membranes, promoting healthy mucus production and further alleviating symptoms of a dry cough.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Fennel decoctions contain mucilages, which are gel-like substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving dryness and irritation.
Expectorants: The volatile oils present in fennel decoctions have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and coughing fits.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and lignans, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat and bronchial tubes, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: A terpene that acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby relieving dry cough symptoms.
Anethole: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate dry cough associated with respiratory conditions.
Dihydroanethole: A phenolic compound that has been shown to exhibit expectorant and mucolytic properties, helping to break down and clear thick mucus, making it easier to cough up.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most used part of Foeniculum vulgare due to their high concentration of volatile oils, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make decoctions for dry cough due to their carminative properties, which help to expel gas and ease digestion.
Stems: Stems are used in decoctions for dry cough because they contain a compound called fenchone, which has a decongestant effect and helps to relieve respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried foeniculum vulgare roots or 1/2 cup of fresh roots in early morning.
Chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare roots into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped foeniculum vulgare roots with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the foeniculum vulgare mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with dry cough because it contains compounds like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate that have natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. When consumed as a warm drink, these compounds help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, providing relief from dry coughs. Additionally, rosemary's stimulating effects on the respiratory system can also help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antitussive property: Rosemary decoction has been shown to relieve dry coughs by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and soothing irritated mucous membranes, thus providing relief from coughing fits.
Expectorant property: Rosemary's expectorant properties help loosen and expel mucus and phlegm from the lungs, allowing for easier elimination of excess secretions that can exacerbate dry coughs.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to the development of chronic dry coughs and other respiratory conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are valued for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Weigh one teaspoon of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Add one cup of water to a saucepan and bring it to boil.
Steep the rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a bowl.
Drink the decoction three times daily, one hour before meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with dry cough because its soothing properties calm irritated throats, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The herb's demulcent qualities create a protective barrier that shields the mucous membranes from further irritation, allowing them to heal naturally. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and chest tightness associated with dry coughs. As a result, mullein decoctions provide effective relief for individuals suffering from persistent, irritating dry coughs.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Mullein's demulcent property helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, providing relief from irritation and inflammation caused by a dry cough.

Expectorant property: The expectorant properties of mullein help to loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel from the lungs and trachea, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with a dry cough.

Anti-inflammatory property: Mullein's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to a dry cough, allowing for easier breathing and reduced coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, roots are effective at soothing and calming dry, irritated respiratory tract tissues.
Leaves: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, leaves help to reduce inflammation and combat infections that contribute to dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried verbascum thapsus leaves and flowers per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured ingredients in a medium-sized saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the mixture to steep and release its medicinal properties.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with dry cough because of its unique combination of compounds that soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The decoction's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, providing relief from the scratchy, persistent discomfort of a dry cough. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties help loosen mucus and promote its expulsion, allowing for easier breathing and a more productive cough. This natural remedy offers a gentle yet effective way to alleviate the symptoms of dry cough.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Yarrow's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, throat, and chest, relieving congestion and dryness associated with dry coughs.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in yarrow soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by dry coughs and promoting healing.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow's antispasmodic properties help relax the smooth muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing spasms and tightness that can exacerbate dry coughs and promote easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, making it effective against dry cough.
Flowers: Containing azulene compounds, they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce irritation and discomfort caused by dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately 20-30 grams of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves.
Combine the measured herb with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water in a large glass container.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an amber-tinted glass bottle in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed decoctions helps with dry cough because its expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up. The herb's antispasmodic properties also soothe and relax the airways, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry coughing fits. Additionally, joe pye weed's natural anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling in the throat and chest, further alleviating dry cough symptoms. As a result, joe pye weed decoctions can provide welcome relief for those struggling with persistent dry coughs.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Joe Pye Weed's decoction helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and dryness that can exacerbate a dry cough.
Anti-inflammatory: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with a dry cough.
Antibacterial: Joe Pye Weed's antibacterial properties help to combat any underlying bacterial infections that may be contributing to the persistence of the cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, Joe Pye Weed roots have been traditionally used to relieve dry cough symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.

Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the plant, Rhyzomes contain a high concentration of medicinal compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and mucus production associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Measure one ounce of dried eupatorium perfoliatum root bark into a clean glass bowl.
Combine the measured herb with four cups of water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil on high heat then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a large glass measuring cup.
Pour the cooled decoction into a clean dark-topped bottle with a tight-fitting lid and store in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with dry cough because it provides a soothing and protective coating to the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, helping to relieve coughing fits and alleviate discomfort. The saponins present in marshmallow roots and leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and irritation, while also increasing the production of mucus to help flush out debris and irritants. This natural remedy helps to calm and comfort the throat, providing fast and effective relief from dry, hacking coughs.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow decoctions contain high amounts of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving inflammation and discomfort associated with dry cough.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and airways, reducing the severity of dry cough symptoms.

Soothing and demulcent properties: The decoction's mucilage content also helps to create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, soothing and calming irritated tissues and providing relief from dryness and discomfort associated with dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots help soothe and calm dry, irritated throats.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the marshmallow plant contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root althaea officinalis fresh or dried for optimal potency.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried marshmallow root or two teaspoons of fresh root for every eight ounces water.
Combine the measured roots with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and store refrigerated for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle decoctions helps with dry cough because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The silymarin present in milk thistle soothes the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation in the throat and bronchial tubes. This allows for easier clearance of mucus and phlegm, alleviating congestion and relieving dryness and irritation. Additionally, milk thistle's natural antitussive properties help to calm persistent coughs, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Milk thistle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as silymarin, help reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve congestion, making it easier to breathe and alleviating dry cough symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of milk thistle decoctions help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dry cough and other respiratory issues.
Mucolytic properties: Milk thistle's mucolytic compounds, such as silibinin, break down thick mucus and phlegm in the airways, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle contain silymarin, a flavonoid that helps to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, providing relief from dry cough.

Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain a unique combination of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the lungs, making them beneficial for dry cough remedy.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried silybum marianum flowers for each cup of boiling water.
Measure and add two cups of filtered water to a medium-sized saucepan.
Boil the water for five minutes, then reduce heat and simmer for ten minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with dry cough because it has a soothing and expectorant property that relaxes the airways, relieving congestion and inflammation. The herb's antiviral and antibacterial compounds help to combat respiratory infections that cause coughing, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce throat irritation and discomfort. As the decoction is inhaled or swallowed, it helps to loosen phlegm and ease the dryness in the throat, providing rapid relief from dry coughs and promoting a good night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Lemon verbena decoctions have been shown to reduce coughing episodes and relieve discomfort due to their ability to suppress the cough reflex.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry coughs.
Expectorant properties: Lemon verbena's expectorant action helps loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and respiratory distress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make decoctions that help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from dry coughs.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, alleviating dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality verbena officinalis leaves and flowers, ensuring a fresh and organic source.
Measure out 20-30 grams of dried or 40-60 grams of fresh verbena officinalis material for each liter of water.
Combine the measured herb material with one liter of boiling water in a large pot, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container, refrigerating it for up to 24 hours before serving in doses of 100-200 milliliters as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with dry cough because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The curcuminoids in turmeric have a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and hacking coughs. Additionally, the decoction's warm and soothing qualities can help to calm irritated throats and reduce inflammation, making it an effective and natural remedy for dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, help reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve congestion associated with dry cough.
Expectorant property: The expectorant properties of turmeric help loosen mucus and phlegm in the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing the severity of a dry cough.

Antimicrobial property: Turmeric's antimicrobial compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections, which are often responsible for dry coughs.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes are commonly used in traditional medicine as they contain curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to relieve dry cough symptoms.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant have been traditionally used to make decoctions for treating respiratory issues like dry cough, due to their ability to soothe and calm irritated airways.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried turmeric root powder and 4 cups of purified water.
Combine the turmeric root powder with the purified water in a medium saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low-medium heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool and refrigerate it for at least 2 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The apigenin and bisabolol compounds in chamomile work to calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and relieving discomfort. As a result, the decoction can help to ease a persistent, hacking cough, providing relief from the scratchiness and irritation associated with dry coughs. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, further contributing to its overall benefits for respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with dry coughs.

Soothing property: The flavonoids and volatile oils present in chamomile have a calming effect on the mucous membranes, which helps to soothe and calm an irritated throat and airways, providing relief from dry coughing.
Expectorant property: Chamomile's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion associated with a dry cough.

Flowers: Chamomile flowers possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and bronchi, providing relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with cold water and soak them in boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers to a cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before consuming it within 30 minutes to an hour after preparation.
Repeat the decoction process twice daily for 2-3 days as needed and adjust dosages accordingly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo decoctions helps with dry cough because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as berberine and hydrastinine, soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and swelling that can exacerbate a dry cough. Additionally, wild indigo's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm, providing relief from congestion and discomfort. By addressing these underlying causes of dry cough, wild indigo decoctions offer effective and natural relief for those suffering from chronic or persistent coughing.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild indigo decoction has antimicrobial properties that help to combat infections caused by bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can contribute to dry coughs.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of dry cough such as soreness and congestion.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, roots are effective at relieving dry cough symptoms by reducing inflammation and congestion.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and tannins, barks help to soothe and protect mucous membranes, alleviating dryness and irritation associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried baptisia tinctoria root per cup of water.
Combine the measured herbs and water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to a simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a large mug.
Drink the warm decoction immediately after preparation while still hot.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry cough that are treated with herbal decoctions are bronchitis, asthma, and respiratory tract infections. Herbal decoctions like ginger, turmeric, and licorice root help soothe irritated airways, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion. They can also relieve symptoms of pharyngitis, laryngitis, and tonsillitis by reducing swelling and killing bacteria that cause infection.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with dry cough is a blend of Slippery Elm, Licorice Root, and Thyme. Slippery Elm soothes the mucous membranes, while Licorice Root helps to reduce inflammation and calm the throat. Thyme adds antibacterial properties, fighting off underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough. Together, these decoctions provide a gentle yet effective treatment for dry, persistent coughs, promoting healing and relief from discomfort. This blend can be taken hot or cold, as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions helps with dry cough because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and respiratory tract, providing relief from dry cough. The decoction also has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up. This, combined with its anti-irritant properties, helps to calm and soothe the throat, providing rapid relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help with dry cough by reducing inflammation in the airways and increasing mucus production to facilitate its removal, thereby alleviating cough symptoms.
Glycyrrhizin: It helps with dry cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the severity of cough episodes, and soothing the throat and airway linings.

Flavonoids (including Isoflavones and Coumarins): They help with dry cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe the airway linings, thereby alleviating cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties to relieve dry cough.
Leaves: Utilized for their secondary metabolites, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects to soothe dry cough.
Roots: Also used for their sweet, licorice-like flavor and aroma, which can help mask the unpleasant taste of other ingredients in cough decoctions.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 5-10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Combine the root with 1 liter of distilled water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and allow the decoction to steep for 30-45 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing it for later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoctions helps with dry cough because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the throat. The decoction's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and coughing. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea contains compounds that stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating the immune system to fight off underlying infections causing the cough. This natural remedy provides relief from dry cough symptoms, promoting a speedy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: Helps with dry cough by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and modulating the immune system, reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Icariin: Helps with dry cough by relaxing the airway muscles, reducing inflammation, and increasing mucociliary clearance, making it easier to cough up mucus.
Alkylphenols (e.g., caffeic acid): Helps with dry cough by exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, reducing the severity of respiratory infections and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used to make decoctions for dry cough because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make decoctions for dry cough because they have antimicrobial properties that can help soothe the throat.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make decoctions for dry cough because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.
Dry the roots and flowers in a single layer at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Measure 1 part of the dried roots and flowers to 4 parts of water by volume in a saucepan.
Combine the measured ingredients in the saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil at 212 degrees fahrenheit.
Simmer the decoction for 5 to 10 minutes to release the active compounds from the echinacea purpurea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus decoctions helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract. The mucilages present in Verbascum thapsus, a type of plant-based glycoprotein, soothe and calm the irritated airways, providing relief from coughing spasms. Additionally, the decoction's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and congestion in the throat, making it easier to breathe and alleviate dry cough symptoms. This natural remedy offers a holistic approach to soothing dry coughs.
remedy_constituents: Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help relieve dry cough by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and promoting the removal of mucus.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Verbascum thapsus have been found to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help ease dry cough by promoting the expulsion of mucus and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Saponins: Saponins in Verbascum thapsus have been found to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help relieve dry cough by reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used in decoctions due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and calm irritated throats.
Stems: The stems are used in decoctions for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which aid in relieving dry cough symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used in decoctions for their soothing and expectorant properties, which help to calm and clear congestion in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves in equal proportions of 10 grams each.
Boil 500 milliliters of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5 minutes.
Add the gathered verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves to the boiling water and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the prepared decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and consume 200 milliliters as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita decoctions helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the airways. The menthol content in Mentha x piperita has a soothing effect on the throat, providing relief from irritation and discomfort associated with dry cough. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of the herb help to relax the muscles in the throat, reducing coughing spasms and promoting a more comfortable breathing experience. This makes it a popular remedy for dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with dry cough by providing a soothing and cooling effect on the mucous membranes, which can help to reduce inflammation and provide temporary relief from coughing.
Menthone: Contributes to the relief of dry cough by acting as a bronchodilator, which helps to open up airways and increase the flow of mucus, making it easier to expel.

Cineole (Eucalyptol): Assists in alleviating dry cough by exhibiting expectorant properties, which helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most used part of Mentha x piperita for making decoctions to treat dry cough due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide expectorant and decongestant properties.
Stems: Stems are also commonly used in decoctions to treat dry cough due to their ability to release menthol and other essential oils that help to relieve cough and congestion.
Roots: Roots are occasionally used in decoctions to treat dry cough due to their store of menthol and other volatile oils that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers on a sunny day after morning dew.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces and let them sit for 5 minutes to release their oils.
Combine the chopped plants with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan and reduce heat to a simmer.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 to 15 minutes to extract the active compounds from the plants.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris decoctions helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties, which facilitate the removal of mucus from the lungs. The active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, found in thyme have natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, reducing congestion and infection that can cause dry cough. Additionally, thymus vulgaris decoctions have a soothing effect on the throat, providing relief from irritation and discomfort associated with dry cough, making it a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: It helps relieve dry cough by exhibiting expectorant properties, making it easier to cough up mucus from the respiratory tract.
Carvacrol: This constituent helps combat dry cough by demonstrating antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce the severity of respiratory infections and soothe irritated airways.
Rosmarinic acid: It aids in alleviating dry cough symptoms by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect the respiratory system and reduce inflammation in the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in thymol, a compound that acts as an expectorant, helping to relieve dry cough.
Stems: The stems of Thymus vulgaris contain thymol and other volatile oils that help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Flowers: The flowers of Thymus vulgaris are used for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which aid in relieving dry cough and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 tablespoons of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers from a healthy plant.
Chop the leaves and flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped thymus vulgaris with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly and cooled.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus decoctions helps with dry cough because it contains compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The main active constituent, eucalyptol, acts as a natural expectorant, loosening and helping to clear mucus from the airways. This helps to reduce congestion and alleviate the dry cough associated with respiratory conditions. As the mucus is cleared, the cough becomes less frequent and less intense, providing relief to the sufferer. Regular consumption of eucalyptus globulus decoctions may also help to soothe the respiratory tract.
remedy_constituents: 1,8-Cineole: This terpene has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from dry cough.
Catechin: As a phenolic compound, catechin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways, alleviating dry cough.
Dihydrocoumarin: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties that help combat infections in the respiratory tract, reducing the severity of dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used to make decoctions for dry cough due to their high eucalyptol content, which has decongestant and expectorant properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used to make decoctions for dry cough as they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory tract congestion.

Stems: Eucalyptus globulus stems can be used to make decoctions for dry cough due to their ability to provide relief from respiratory issues, including cough and congestion, through the release of volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in quantities of 2-3 tablespoons per cup of water.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the eucalyptus globulus leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to separate the solids from the liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale decoctions helps with dry cough because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, making it easier to cough up mucus. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to soothe the throat and calm irritation. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, providing relief from dry coughs. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to ease respiratory issues and promote overall health. Its benefits make it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to a dry cough by decreasing irritation and swelling in the throat and lungs.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate dry cough symptoms by reducing inflammation and discomfort in the respiratory system.
Terpinen-4-ol: This terpene has expectorant properties, helping to thin and loosen mucus in the airways, making it easier to expel and potentially relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the plant are also rich in active compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and soothing dry cough.
Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are believed to possess medicinal properties that help in relieving dry cough and other respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-6 fresh rhizomes of zingiber officinale with clean scissors to avoid contamination.
Wash the harvested rhizomes with cold running water to remove dirt and debris immediately.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and soak them in 2 cups of water for 2 hours.
Boil the soaked rhizome mixture in a saucepan with 4 cups of water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove solids immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions helps with dry cough because of its expectorant properties that loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs. This herbal remedy also improves blood circulation and oxygenation to the respiratory system, reducing dryness and congestion. As a result, Ginkgo biloba decoctions provide relief from dry cough and promote a more comfortable respiratory experience.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to dry cough.

Terpene lactones: Specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolides, these terpene lactones have anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing the severity of dry cough.

Bilobalane: Although less studied than other constituents, bilobalane has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate dry cough symptoms by protecting the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for the medicinal properties of Ginkgo biloba.
Seeds: They are used because they contain ginkgolic acid, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help relieve dry cough.
Barks: They are used due to their ability to provide a soothing and protective effect on the respiratory tract, which helps to alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves or 30 grams of dried leaves from mature trees.
Chop the leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia decoctions helps with dry cough because they contain compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, making it easier to cough up mucus. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can soothe a sore throat and promote relaxation, which can help to alleviate the discomfort associated with dry cough. As a result, Lavandula angustifolia decoctions can provide natural relief from dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps to relax the muscles in the throat, reducing inflammation and spasms that contribute to dry cough.

Linalyl acetate: An ester that exhibits anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and promote the clearance of mucus that may be exacerbating dry cough.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways, alleviating symptoms of dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help relieve dry cough.

Flowers: They are used because of their high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing dry cough.
Stems: They are used as a source of essential oils and other bioactive compounds that help to relax the airways and relieve dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the freshly harvested lavandula angustifolia flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 week.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and combine with 250ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes then strain the liquid using a cheesecloth.
Store the cooled lavandula angustifolia decoction in a dark glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra decoctions helps with dry cough because they contain high levels of antiviral compounds, particularly isoquercitrin, which helps combat viral infections causing the cough. The decoction's rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids also aids in reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes, relieving the discomfort associated with dry cough. Additionally, Sambucus nigra's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up and alleviate the dry, hacking sensation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating dry cough symptoms.
Phenolic Acids: They exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat underlying viral infections causing dry cough.
Triterpenoids (specifically Ursolic Acid): Ursolic acid has expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus and make it easier to expel, relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make decoctions for dry cough due to their antitussive (cough-suppressing) properties.
Fruits: The fruits, specifically elderberries, are used to make decoctions for dry cough as they contain flavonoids and anthocyanins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Leaves: They are used to make decoctions for dry cough due to their antiseptic and expectorant properties that help relieve congestion and cough.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30-60g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves or dried flowers and leaves.
Combine the gathered flowers and leaves in a saucepan and add 1-2 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Steep the mixture for 10-30 minutes or until the desired concentration is achieved.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to obtain the final herbal remedy.
